title: Overview & Legal Framework
subtitle: Scope, definitions, classification of heritage structures
content: Establishes the legal basis, definitions, and classification system for Yemen's built heritage. Defines four heritage grades (A–D), geographic scope across all 22 governorates, relationship to existing municipal codes, and the authority of the YAI committee for variance applications.
specs: A: National significance · B: Regional · C: Local · D: Contributory
All structures in classified heritage zones or within 50m of Grade A/B building
YAI Heritage Code Committee — technical review within 45 days
YBC-H supersedes SBC 902 for classified Yemeni heritage structures
traditional: All restoration or reconstruction of Grade A and B structures must comply with YBC-H before any municipal code.
transitional: Grade C and D structures may apply hybrid compliance — YBC-H material standards + municipal structural code.
contemporary: New construction in heritage zones must demonstrate character compliance per YAI character card for the governorate.
